title: The Little Raccoon Who Learned to Share

illustrations.alt: Ruby the raccoon discovers a glowing rainbow pebble by the sparkling forest stream, her eyes wide with wonder as sunlight dances on the water
Ruby sits alone in her cozy tree hollow surrounded by her collection of treasures, looking sad and lonely while the colorful pebble sits in a box nearby
Ruby the raccoon joyfully shows the glowing rainbow pebble to Bella the bunny under a beautiful sunset sky, both friends smiling with wonder and happiness

moral: When you share what you have, joy doesn't split — it multiplies. The best treasures are the ones that make everyone smile together.
moralZh: 分享并不会让快乐减少，反而会让快乐加倍。最好的宝贝，是那些能让所有人都一起微笑的东西。

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: In a sun-dappled corner of Brightwood Forest, where the trees whispered secrets and the stream hummed a cheerful tune, there lived a little raccoon named Ruby.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby had the bushiest tail in the forest — striped with silver and gray like rings on a wizard's staff — and the brightest, most curious eyes you could ever see. But Ruby also had a very special collection.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Inside the hollow of an old oak tree, Ruby kept her secret treasure chest — an old wooden box painted with acorns and leaves. Inside it were: three shiny bottle caps, a silver button that caught the light, two smooth river stones, and the feather of a blue jay.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby loved her treasures. She loved them so much that she never showed them to anyone — not even to Bella, her best friend in the whole forest.

paragraphs.speaker: Bella
paragraphs.text: "Ruby! Ruby! Come quick! There's something shiny in the stream!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Bella the Bunny came hopping through the ferns, her ears flopping excitedly. Ruby scrambled down from her tree and followed Bella to the edge of Sparkling Stream.

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "Where? What is it? I love shiny things!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The two friends peered into the clear, rushing water. And there, half-buried in the pebbles at the bottom, something glowed with all the colors of the rainbow.

paragraphs.speaker: Bella
paragraphs.text: "It's the most beautiful pebble I've ever seen! It's like a tiny rainbow trapped in a stone! Let me try to get it for you!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But Ruby was faster. She plunged her little paws into the chilly water, grabbed the rainbow pebble, and held it up to the sunlight. The pebble sparkled and shimmered, casting tiny rainbows across the grass.

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "Wow... It's MINE now! This is the best treasure I've ever found!"

paragraphs.speaker: Bella
paragraphs.text: "Can I see it? Just for a second?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby hesitated. She clutched the pebble tightly against her chest. Something inside her whispered: 'Don't let go. It's yours. Yours alone.'

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "Um... maybe later, Bella. I need to clean it first. And put it in a safe place. You know — special pebble care!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby scurried away before Bella could say another word. She climbed up to her tree hollow, took out her treasure box, and carefully placed the rainbow pebble on top of everything else.

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "There! Now you're safe. My most beautiful treasure. All mine."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby closed the box and sat back, expecting to feel wonderful. But somehow... she didn't. The hollow felt darker than usual. Quieter. Lonelier.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The next morning, Ruby opened her box just to peek at the pebble. It still sparkled. But the sparkle didn't reach her heart the way it had by the stream.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: She heard laughter outside. She peeked through the leaves and saw Bella playing with Ollie the Owl near the old berry bush. Bella was showing Ollie how to do a new hop-jump she had invented.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby felt a strange, twisty feeling in her tummy. She held the rainbow pebble tighter. But the tighter she held it, the heavier it felt.

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "Why aren't you making me happy anymore?" she whispered to the pebble. The pebble, of course, did not answer.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Days passed. Ruby stayed in her hollow, looking at her treasures, while Bella played outside with the other forest friends. The rainbow pebble sat in its box, beautiful and completely alone — just like Ruby.

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Ruby? Ruby, are you in there?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: It was Ollie the Owl. His big golden eyes blinked patiently at the entrance of Ruby's hollow.

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "Go away, Ollie. I'm busy guarding my treasures."

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Guarding them? Or hiding from us?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby's ears drooped. Ollie had a way of asking questions that poked right through her fuzzy fur and into her feelings.

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "It's just... I found this beautiful pebble. The most beautiful one in the whole forest. And I was so happy at first. But now... I don't feel happy at all. I feel lonely."

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Ruby, can I tell you something I've learned? I've been watching sunsets for many years. The most beautiful sunset in the world is not the one you watch alone — it's the one you watch with someone who says 'wow' at the exact same moment."

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "What do you mean?"

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "I mean that the best treasures aren't the ones you keep — they're the ones you share. A treasure that nobody else sees is just a rock in a box. But a treasure that makes someone else's eyes light up? That's magic."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby looked down at the pebble. It was still beautiful. But Ollie's words shimmered even brighter than the pebble's colors.

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "You really think so?"

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "I know so. Try it. Show it to Bella. I bet her eyes will light up like fireflies on a summer night."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby took a deep breath. She picked up the rainbow pebble, still warm from being clutched in her paws, and climbed down from her tree. Her heart thumped like a drum.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: She found Bella sitting by the stream, staring at the spot where they had first found the pebble together. Bella looked up when Ruby approached.

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "Bella... I'm sorry. I should have shown you this the day we found it. Here."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ruby held out her paw. The rainbow pebble sat in the middle of it, glowing softly in the afternoon light. Bella's eyes grew wide.

paragraphs.speaker: Bella
paragraphs.text: "Oh, Ruby! It's even more beautiful than I remembered! It's like holding a piece of the rainbow!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And then it happened. Bella's face lit up with the biggest, brightest smile Ruby had ever seen. And right at that moment, Ruby felt something she hadn't felt in days — true happiness. It surged through her like warm honey.

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "You know what? I want you to have it. For real. I want you to take it home and keep it for a while."

paragraphs.speaker: Bella
paragraphs.text: "Ruby! I can't! It's your treasure!"

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "No — I think the treasure was always meant to be both of ours. Besides, watching you smile is worth more than any pebble. And you can show it to Ollie and everyone else too!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: That evening, Ruby, Bella, and Ollie gathered by the old oak tree. Bella showed the rainbow pebble to everyone who passed by. Each time someone new looked at it, their face lit up, and Ruby felt her heart grow a little warmer.

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "See, Ruby? The pebble didn't stop being beautiful when you shared it. It became MORE beautiful — because now it carries a hundred happy looks, not just one."

paragraphs.speaker: Ruby
paragraphs.text: "You were right, Ollie. Sharing doesn't make a treasure smaller. It makes the treasure... bigger. And so does the happiness."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: That night, Ruby went back to her hollow and opened her treasure box. For the first time, she didn't feel the urge to close it and hide it away. Instead, she smiled and thought about all the things she could share tomorrow.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And here's the beautiful secret Ruby discovered: when you share something, you don't lose it. You just get to enjoy it through everyone else's eyes too. And somehow, that makes it a thousand times more precious.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: So remember, whenever you find something wonderful — a toy, a book, a snack, or a rainbow pebble — think of Ruby the Raccoon. Because the best way to keep a treasure forever is to let someone else love it too.
